title: Output Formatting and Colors
content:
Well-formatted output makes CLI tools pleasant to use. Support multiple output formats (human-readable tables, JSON for machine consumption) and use colors judiciously.

The convention: if stdout is a terminal, use colors and pretty formatting. If piped, output plain text. Rust crates like colored, console, and tabled help with this.

Always output data to stdout and status/errors to stderr. This lets users pipe data while still seeing progress.

codeExamples.description: Conditional formatting based on output target

keyPoints: Data goes to stdout, status/errors to stderr
Support both human-readable and machine-readable (JSON) output
Check is_terminal() to decide whether to use colors/formatting
Use format specifiers for aligned output: {:<width$}, {:>width$}
Print progress to stderr so it does not corrupt piped output
